revision.text: #redirect[[en:癸]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{十干}} '''癸'''（き、みずのと）は、[[十干]]の10番目である。[[陰陽五行説]]では[[水]]性の陰に割り当てられており、ここから日本では「みずのと」（水の弟）ともいう。
癸の字は「揆」（はかる）につながり、植物の内部にできた種子が大きさを測れるまで大きくなった状態として、10番目に宛てられた。

{{stub}} [[Category:干支|みすのと]]

癸の字は「揆」（はかる）につながり、植物の内部にできた種子が大きさを測れるまで大きくなった状態として、10番目に宛てられた。

{{十干}} '''癸'''（き、みずのと）は、[[十干]]の10番目である。[[陰陽五行説]]では[[水]]性の陰に割り当てられており、ここから日本では「みずのと」（水の弟）ともいう。
癸の字は「揆」（はかる）につながり、植物の内部にできた種子が大きさを測れるまで大きくなった状態として、10番目に宛てられた。
[[中国語]]の[[化合物]]命名法では、[[デカン]]、[[デシル基]]など、[[炭素]]を10含む化合物や[[基|官能基]]の名に付けられる。ただし、[[ビタミン]]などの名称ではKの代わりに使われている。

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Gŭi (tiĕng-găng)]][[commons:Category:癸 (Heavenly Stem)]][[fr:Gui (tige céleste)]][[gan:癸]][[hak:Kui (thiên-kôn)]][[ja:癸]][[ko:계 (천간)]][[vi:Quý (Thiên can)]][[wuu:癸]][[za:Gveiq (diengan)]][[zh_min_nan:Kúi (Thian-kan)]][[zh_yue:癸]][[zh:癸]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1078044]] Wikidata name: guǐ [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Mizunoto''' (癸) is the 10th of the [[Ten Heavenly Stems]] (十干).<ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=56510&element_id=73054 <nowiki>Entry Details for 癸 [mizunoto] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=7c8f1fd0ee8d4469933a9f79651d6c92eea130421c8e1d4cc1e2c7a8335b6d1fJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=30e11164-4621-64c3-1cab-04d947776577&psq=Mizunoto+(%e7%99%b8)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU2MzU0L2tpLW1penVub3RvLSVFNyU5OSVCOC0lRTMlODElOEQtJUUzJTgxJUJGJUUzJTgxJTlBJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJUE4&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 癸 (Stem 10) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Guǐ * Korean: Gye (계) * Vietnamese: Quý * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoto (みずのと, 水の弟 – “Younger Water”)
== Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the [[:en:Water|Water]] element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chinese|Chinese]] chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten [[:en:carbon|carbon]] atoms, such as [[:en:decane|decane]] and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Bō͘ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|zh|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}. * The auspicious direction (Eho) associated with Kui is slightly south of due southeast (165 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoto (癸)== {{wiktionary|癸}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Water <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Gŭi (tiĕng-găng)]][[sn:commons:Category:癸 (Heavenly Stem)]][[fr:Gui (tige céleste)]][[gan:癸]][[hak:Kui (thiên-kôn)]][[ja:癸]][[ko:계 (천간)]][[vi:Quý (Thiên can)]][[wuu:癸]][[za:Gveiq (diengan)]][[zh min nan:Kúi (Thian-kan)]][[zh_yue:癸]][[zh:癸]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1078044]] Wikidata name: guǐ [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Mizunoto''' (癸) is the 10th of the [[Ten Heavenly Stems]] (十干).<ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=56510&element_id=73054 <nowiki>Entry Details for 癸 [mizunoto] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=7c8f1fd0ee8d4469933a9f79651d6c92eea130421c8e1d4cc1e2c7a8335b6d1fJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=30e11164-4621-64c3-1cab-04d947776577&psq=Mizunoto+(%e7%99%b8)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU2MzU0L2tpLW1penVub3RvLSVFNyU5OSVCOC0lRTMlODElOEQtJUUzJTgxJUJGJUUzJTgxJTlBJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJUE4&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 癸 (Stem 10) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Guǐ * Korean: Gye (계) * Vietnamese: Quý * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoto (みずのと, 水の弟 – “Younger Water”)
== Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the [[:en:Water|Water]] element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chinese|Chinese]] chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten [[:en:carbon|carbon]] atoms, such as [[:en:decane|decane]] and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Bō͘ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|zh|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}. * The auspicious direction (Eho) associated with Kui is slightly south of due southeast (165 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoto (癸)== {{wiktionary|癸}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Water == Pronunciations: 癸 (Stem 10) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Guǐ * Korean: Gye (계) * Vietnamese: Quý * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoto (みずのと, 水の弟 – “Younger Water”)
== Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the [[:en:Water|Water]] element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chinese|Chinese]] chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten [[:en:carbon|carbon]] atoms, such as [[:en:decane|decane]] and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Bō͘ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Gŭi (tiĕng-găng)]][[sn:commons:Category:癸 (Heavenly Stem)]][[fr:Gui (tige céleste)]][[gan:癸]][[hak:Kui (thiên-kôn)]][[ja:癸]][[ko:계 (천간)]][[vi:Quý (Thiên can)]][[wuu:癸]][[za:Gveiq (diengan)]][[zh-min-nan:Kúi (Thian-kan)]][[zh-yue:癸]][[zh:癸]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078044]] Wikidata name: guǐ [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Mizunoto''' (癸) is the 10th of the [[Ten Heavenly Stems]] (十干).<ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=56510&element_id=73054 <nowiki>Entry Details for 癸 [mizunoto] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=7c8f1fd0ee8d4469933a9f79651d6c92eea130421c8e1d4cc1e2c7a8335b6d1fJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=30e11164-4621-64c3-1cab-04d947776577&psq=Mizunoto+(%e7%99%b8)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU2MzU0L2tpLW1penVub3RvLSVFNyU5OSVCOC0lRTMlODElOEQtJUUzJTgxJUJGJUUzJTgxJTlBJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJUE4&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 癸 (Stem 10) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Guǐ * Korean: Gye (계) * Vietnamese: Quý * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoto (みずのと, 水の弟 – “Younger Water”)
== Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the [[:en:Water|Water]] element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chinese|Chinese]] chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten [[:en:carbon|carbon]] atoms, such as [[:en:decane|decane]] and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Bō͘ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|zh|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}. * The auspicious direction (Eho) associated with Kui is slightly south of due southeast (165 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoto (癸)== {{wiktionary|癸}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Water Rooster|ja|癸酉|1=Yin Water Rooster|2=ja|3=癸酉|4=zh|5-癸酉|6=ko|7=계유|lt_ko=계유|8=fr|9=Coq de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Hahn|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rooster|zh|癸酉|1=Yin Water == Pronunciations: 癸 (Stem 10) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Guǐ * Korean: Gye (계) * Vietnamese: Quý * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoto (みずのと, 水の弟 – “Younger Water”)
== Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the [[:en:Water|Water]] element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chinese|Chinese]] chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten [[:en:carbon|carbon]] atoms, such as [[:en:decane|decane]] and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Bō͘ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|zh|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}. * The auspicious direction (Eho) associated with Kui is slightly south of due southeast (165 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoto (癸)== {{wiktionary|癸}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Water Rooster|ja|癸酉|1=Yin Water Rooster|2=ja|3=癸酉|4=zh|5-癸酉|6=ko|7=계유|lt_ko=계유|8=fr|9=Coq de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Hahn|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rooster|zh|癸酉|1=Yin Water Rooster|2=ja|3=癸酉|4=zh|5-癸酉|6=ko|7=계유|lt_ko=계유|8=fr|9=Coq de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Hahn|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rooster|ko|계유|fr|Coq de eau|de|Wasser-Hahn|en|Water Rooster|lt=癸酉 (Yin Water Rooster)|lt_ko=癸酉 (계유)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rooster|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;" |{{ill|Yin Water Goat|ja|1=Yin Water Goat|2=ja|癸未|1=Yin Water Goat|zh|1=Yin Water Goat|2=ja|癸未|1=Yin Water Goat|ko|계미|fr|Chèvre de eau|de|Wasser-Schaf|en|Water Goat|lt=癸未 (Yin Water Goat)|lt_ko=癸未 (계미)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Goat|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Goat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;" |{{ill|Yin Water Snake|ja|癸巳|zh|癸巳|ko|계사 (간지)|fr|Serpent de eau|de|Wasser-Schlange|en|Water Snake|lt=癸巳 (Yin Water Snake)|lt_ko=癸巳 (계사)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Snake|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |- style="text-align:left;" |{{ill|Yin Water Rabbit|ja|1=Yin Water Rabbit|2=ja|癸卯|1=Yin Water Rabbit|zh|1=Yin Water Rabbit|2=ja|癸卯|1=Yin Water Rabbit|ko|계묘|fr|Lièvre de eau|de|Wasser-Hase|en|Water Rabbit|lt=癸卯 (Yin Water Rabbit)|lt_ko=癸卯 (계묘)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rabbit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;" |{{ill|Yin Water Ox|ja|1=Yin Water Ox|2=ja|癸丑|zh|1=Yin Water Ox|2=ja|癸丑|ko|계축|fr|Buffle de eau|de|Wasser-Büffel|en|Water Ox|lt=癸丑 (Yin Water Ox)|lt_ko=癸丑 (계축)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Ox|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;" |{{ill|Yin Water Pig|ja|1=Yin Water Pig|2=ja|癸亥|zh|1=Yin Water Pig|2=ja|癸亥|ko|계해|fr|Cochon de eau|de|Wasser-Schwein|en|Water Pig|lt=癸亥 (Yin Water Pig)|lt_ko=癸亥 (계해)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Pig|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |}

== Pronunciations: 癸 (Stem 10) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Guǐ * Korean: Gye (계) * Vietnamese: Quý * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoto (みずのと, 水の弟 – “Younger Water”)
== Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the [[:en:Water|Water]] element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chinese|Chinese]] chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten [[:en:carbon|carbon]] atoms, such as [[:en:decane|decane]] and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Bō͘ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|zh|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}. * The auspicious direction (Eho) associated with Kui is slightly south of due southeast (165 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoto (癸)== {{wiktionary|癸}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Water Rooster|ja|癸酉|1=Yin Water <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Gŭi (tiĕng-găng)]][[sn:commons:Category:癸 (Heavenly Stem)]][[fr:Gui (tige céleste)]][[gan:癸]][[hak:Kui (thiên-kôn)]][[ja:癸]][[ko:계 (천간)]][[vi:Quý (Thiên can)]][[wuu:癸]][[za:Gveiq (diengan)]][[zh-min-nan:Kúi (Thian-kan)]][[zh-yue:癸]][[zh:癸]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078044]] Wikidata name: guǐ [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Mizunoto''' (癸) is the 10th of the [[Ten Heavenly Stems]] (十干).<ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=56510&element_id=73054 <nowiki>Entry Details for 癸 [mizunoto] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=7c8f1fd0ee8d4469933a9f79651d6c92eea130421c8e1d4cc1e2c7a8335b6d1fJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=30e11164-4621-64c3-1cab-04d947776577&psq=Mizunoto+(%e7%99%b8)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU2MzU0L2tpLW1penVub3RvLSVFNyU5OSVCOC0lRTMlODElOEQtJUUzJTgxJUJGJUUzJTgxJTlBJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJUE4&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 癸 (Stem 10) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Guǐ * Korean: Gye (계) * Vietnamese: Quý * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoto (みずのと, 水の弟 – “Younger Water”)
== Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the [[:en:Water|Water]] element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chinese|Chinese]] chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten [[:en:carbon|carbon]] atoms, such as [[:en:decane|decane]] and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Bō͘ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|zh|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}. * The auspicious direction (Eho) associated with Kui is slightly south of due southeast (165 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoto (癸)== {{wiktionary|癸}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Water Rooster|ja|癸酉|1=Yin Water This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chinese|Chinese]] chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten [[:en:carbon|carbon]] atoms, such as [[:en:decane|decane]] and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Bō͘ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|zh|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}. * The auspicious direction (Eho) associated with Kui is slightly south of due southeast (165 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoto (癸)== {{wiktionary|癸}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Water Rooster|ja|癸酉|1=Yin Water Rooster|2=ja|3=癸酉|4=zh|5-癸酉|6=ko|7=계유|lt_ko=계유|8=fr|9=Coq de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Hahn|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rooster|zh|癸酉|1=Yin Water Rooster|2=ja|3=癸酉|4=zh|5-癸酉|6=ko|7=계유|lt_ko=계유|8=fr|9=Coq de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Hahn|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rooster|ko|계유|fr|Coq de eau|de|Wasser-Hahn|en|Water Rooster|lt=癸酉 (Yin Goat|2=ja|癸未|1=Yin Water Goat|ko|계미|fr|Chèvre de eau|de|Wasser-Schaf|en|Water Goat|lt=癸未 (Yin Water Goat)|lt_ko=癸未 (계미)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Goat|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water (계사)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Snake|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |- style="text-align:left;" |{{ill|Yin Water Rabbit|ja|1=Yin Water Rabbit|2=ja|癸卯|1=Yin Water Rabbit|zh|1=Yin Water Ox|lt=癸丑 (Yin Water Ox)|lt_ko=癸丑 (계축)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Ox|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;" |{{ill|Yin Water Pig|ja|1=Yin Water Pig|2=ja|癸亥|zh|1=Yin == Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the [[:en:Water|Water]] element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] correspond to Kui years. [[:en:decane|decane]] and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Gŭi (tiĕng-găng)]][[sn:commons:Category:癸 (Heavenly Stem)]][[fr:Gui (tige céleste)]][[gan:癸]][[hak:Kui (thiên-kôn)]][[ja:癸]][[ko:계 (천간)]][[vi:Quý (Thiên can)]][[wuu:癸]][[za:Gveiq (diengan)]][[zh-min-nan:Kúi (Thian-kan)]][[zh-yue:癸]][[zh:癸]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078044]] Wikidata name: guǐ [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Mizunoto''' (癸) is the 10th of the [[Ten Heavenly Stems]] (十干).<ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=56510&element_id=73054 <nowiki>Entry Details for 癸 [mizunoto] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=7c8f1fd0ee8d4469933a9f79651d6c92eea130421c8e1d4cc1e2c7a8335b6d1fJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=30e11164-4621-64c3-1cab-04d947776577&psq=Mizunoto+(%e7%99%b8)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU2MzU0L2tpLW1penVub3RvLSVFNyU5OSVCOC0lRTMlODElOEQtJUUzJTgxJUJGJUUzJTgxJTlBJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJUE4&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 癸 (Stem 10) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Guǐ * Korean: Gye (계) * Vietnamese: Quý * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoto (みずのと, 水の弟 – “Younger Water”)
== Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the [[:en:Water|Water]] element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chinese|Chinese]] chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten [[:en:carbon|carbon]] atoms, such as [[:en:decane|decane]] and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Bō͘ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|zh|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}. * The auspicious direction (Eho) associated with Kui is slightly south of due southeast (165 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoto (癸)== {{wiktionary|癸}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Water Rooster|ja|癸酉|1=Yin Water Rooster|2=ja|3=癸酉|4=zh|5-癸酉|6=ko|7=계유|lt_ko=계유|8=fr|9=Coq de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Hahn|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rooster|zh|癸酉|1=Yin Water Rooster|2=ja|3=癸酉|4=zh|5-癸酉|6=ko|7=계유|lt_ko=계유|8=fr|9=Coq de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Hahn|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rooster|ko|계유|fr|Coq de eau|de|Wasser-Hahn|en|Water Rooster|lt=癸酉 (Yin Water Rooster)|lt_ko=癸酉 (계유)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rooster|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;" |{{ill|Yin Water Goat|ja|1=Yin Water Goat|2=ja|癸未|1=Yin Water Goat|zh|1=Yin Water Goat|2=ja|癸未|1=Yin Water Goat|ko|계미|fr|Chèvre de eau|de|Wasser-Schaf|en|Water Goat|lt=癸未 (Yin Water Goat)|lt_ko=癸未 (계미)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Goat|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Goat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;" |{{ill|Yin Water Snake|ja|癸巳|zh|癸巳|ko|계사 (간지)|fr|Serpent de eau|de|Wasser-Schlange|en|Water Snake|lt=癸巳 (Yin Water Snake)|lt_ko=癸巳 (계사)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Snake|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |- style="text-align:left;" |{{ill|Yin Water Rabbit|ja|1=Yin Water Rabbit|2=ja|癸卯|1=Yin Water Rabbit|zh|1=Yin Water Rabbit|2=ja|癸卯|1=Yin Water Rabbit|ko|계묘|fr|Lièvre de eau|de|Wasser-Hase|en|Water Rabbit|lt=癸卯 (Yin Water Rabbit)|lt_ko=癸卯 (계묘)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rabbit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;" |{{ill|Yin Water Ox|ja|1=Yin Water Ox|2=ja|癸丑|zh|1=Yin Water Ox|2=ja|癸丑|ko|계축|fr|Buffle de eau|de|Wasser-Büffel|en|Water Ox|lt=癸丑 (Yin Water Ox)|lt_ko=癸丑 (계축)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Ox|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;" |{{ill|Yin Water Pig|ja|1=Yin Water Pig|2=ja|癸亥|zh|1=Yin Water Pig|2=ja|癸亥|ko|계해|fr|Cochon de eau|de|Wasser-Schwein|en|Water Pig|lt=癸亥 (Yin Water Pig)|lt_ko=癸亥 (계해)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Pig|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |}

[[:en:decane|decane]] and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Gŭi (tiĕng-găng)]][[sn:commons:Category:癸 (Heavenly Stem)]][[fr:Gui (tige céleste)]][[gan:癸]][[hak:Kui (thiên-kôn)]][[ja:癸]][[ko:계 (천간)]][[vi:Quý (Thiên can)]][[wuu:癸]][[za:Gveiq (diengan)]][[zh-min-nan:Kúi (Thian-kan)]][[zh-yue:癸]][[zh:癸]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078044]] Wikidata name: guǐ [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Mizunoto''' (癸) is the 10th of the [[Ten Heavenly Stems]] (十干).<ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=56510&element_id=73054 <nowiki>Entry Details for 癸 [mizunoto] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=7c8f1fd0ee8d4469933a9f79651d6c92eea130421c8e1d4cc1e2c7a8335b6d1fJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=30e11164-4621-64c3-1cab-04d947776577&psq=Mizunoto+(%e7%99%b8)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU2MzU0L2tpLW1penVub3RvLSVFNyU5OSVCOC0lRTMlODElOEQtJUUzJTgxJUJGJUUzJTgxJTlBJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJUE4&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 癸 (Stem 10) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Guǐ * Korean: Gye (계) * Vietnamese: Quý * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoto (みずのと, 水の弟 – “Younger Water”)
== Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the [[:en:Water|Water]] element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chinese|Chinese]] chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten [[:en:carbon|carbon]] atoms, such as [[:en:decane|decane]] and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Bō͘ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|zh|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}. * The auspicious direction (Eho) associated with Kui is This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chinese|Chinese]] chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten [[:en:carbon|carbon]] atoms, such as [[:en:decane|decane]] and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Bō͘ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|zh|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}. * The auspicious direction (Eho) associated with Kui is slightly south of due southeast (165 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoto (癸)== {{wiktionary|癸}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Water Rooster|ja|癸酉|1=Yin Water Rooster|2=ja|3=癸酉|4=zh|5-癸酉|6=ko|7=계유|lt_ko=계유|8=fr|9=Coq de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Hahn|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rooster|zh|癸酉|1=Yin Water == Pronunciations: 癸 (Stem 10) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Guǐ * Korean: Gye (계) * Vietnamese: Quý * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoto (みずのと, 水の弟 – “Younger Water”)
== Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the [[:en:Water|Water]] element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chinese|Chinese]] chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten [[:en:carbon|carbon]] atoms, such as [[:en:decane|decane]] and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Bō͘ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|zh|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}. * The auspicious direction (Eho) associated with Kui is slightly south of due southeast (165 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoto (癸)== {{wiktionary|癸}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Water Rooster|ja|癸酉|1=Yin Water == Pronunciations: 癸 (Stem 10) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Guǐ * Korean: Gye (계) * Vietnamese: Quý * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoto (みずのと, 水の弟 – “Younger Water”)
== Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the [[:en:Water|Water]] element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chinese|Chinese]] chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten [[:en:carbon|carbon]] atoms, such as [[:en:decane|decane]] and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Bō͘ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|zh|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}. * The auspicious direction (Eho) associated with Kui is slightly south of due southeast (165 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoto (癸)== {{wiktionary|癸}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Water Rooster|ja|癸酉|1=Yin Water == Pronunciations: 癸 (Stem 10) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Guǐ * Korean: Gye (계) * Vietnamese: Quý * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoto (みずのと, 水の弟 – “Younger Water”)
== Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the [[:en:Water|Water]] element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chinese|Chinese]] chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten [[:en:carbon|carbon]] atoms, such as [[:en:decane|decane]] and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Bō͘ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|zh|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}. * The auspicious direction (Eho) associated with Kui is == Pronunciations: 癸 (Stem 10) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Guǐ * Korean: Gye (계) * Vietnamese: Quý * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoto (みずのと, 水の弟 – “Younger Water”)
== Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the [[:en:Water|Water]] element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chinese|Chinese]] chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten [[:en:carbon|carbon]] atoms, such as [[:en:decane|decane]] and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Bō͘ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|zh|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}. * The auspicious direction (Eho) associated with Kui is slightly south of due southeast (165 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoto (癸)== {{wiktionary|癸}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Water Rooster|ja|癸酉|1=Yin Water == Pronunciations: 癸 (Stem 10) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Guǐ * Korean: Gye (계) * Vietnamese: Quý * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoto (みずのと, 水の弟 – “Younger Water”)
== Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the [[:en:Water|Water]] element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chinese|Chinese]] chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten [[:en:carbon|carbon]] atoms, such as [[:en:decane|decane]] and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe == Pronunciations: 癸 (Stem 10) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Guǐ * Korean: Gye (계) * Vietnamese: Quý * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoto (みずのと, 水の弟 – “Younger Water”)
== Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the [[:en:Water|Water]] element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chinese|Chinese]] chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten [[:en:carbon|carbon]] atoms, such as [[:en:decane|decane]] and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Bō͘ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|zh|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}. * The auspicious direction (Eho) associated with Kui is slightly south of due southeast (165 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoto (癸)== {{wiktionary|癸}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Water Rooster|ja|癸酉|1=Yin Water Water Rooster)|lt_ko=癸酉 (계유)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rooster|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;" |{{ill|Yin Water Goat|ja|1=Yin Water Goat|2=ja|癸未|1=Yin Water Goat|zh|1=Yin Water Goat|2=ja|癸未|1=Yin Water Goat|ko|계미|fr|Chèvre de eau|de|Wasser-Schaf|en|Water Goat|lt=癸未 (Yin Water Goat)|lt_ko=癸未 (계미)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Goat|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water == Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the {{ill|Water|en|Water|WD=Q283|lt=Water}} element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese|en|Chinese|WD=Q1173004|lt=Chinese}} chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten {{ill|carbon|en|carbon|WD=Q623|lt=carbon}} atoms, such as {{ill|decane|en|decane|WD=Q150717|lt=decane}} and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is == Pronunciations: 癸 (Stem 10) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Guǐ * Korean: Gye (계) * Vietnamese: Quý * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoto (みずのと, 水の弟 – “Younger Water”)
== Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the {{ill|Water|en|Water|WD=Q283|lt=Water}} element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese|en|Chinese|WD=Q1173004|lt=Chinese}} chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten {{ill|carbon|en|carbon|WD=Q623|lt=carbon}} atoms, such as {{ill|decane|en|decane|WD=Q150717|lt=decane}} and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Bō͘ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|zh|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}. * The auspicious direction (Eho) associated with Kui is slightly south of due southeast (165 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoto (癸)== {{wiktionary|癸}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Water Rooster|ja|癸酉|1=Yin Water Rooster|2=ja|3=癸酉|4=zh|5-癸酉|6=ko|7=계유|lt_ko=계유|8=fr|9=Coq de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Hahn|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rooster|zh|癸酉|1=Yin Water Rooster|2=ja|3=癸酉|4=zh|5-癸酉|6=ko|7=계유|lt_ko=계유|8=fr|9=Coq de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Hahn|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rooster|ko|계유|fr|Coq de eau|de|Wasser-Hahn|en|Water Rooster|lt=癸酉 (Yin Water Rooster)|lt_ko=癸酉 (계유)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin (계축)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Ox|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;" |{{ill|Yin Water Pig|ja|1=Yin Water Pig|2=ja|癸亥|zh|1=Yin Water Pig|2=ja|癸亥|ko|계해|fr|Cochon de eau|de|Wasser-Schwein|en|Water Pig|lt=癸亥 (Yin Water Pig)|lt_ko=癸亥 (계해)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Pig|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |}

{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Mizunoto''' (癸) is the 10th of the [[Ten Heavenly Stems]] (十干).<ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=56510&element_id=73054 <nowiki>Entry Details for 癸 [mizunoto] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=7c8f1fd0ee8d4469933a9f79651d6c92eea130421c8e1d4cc1e2c7a8335b6d1fJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=30e11164-4621-64c3-1cab-04d947776577&psq=Mizunoto+(%e7%99%b8)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU2MzU0L2tpLW1penVub3RvLSVFNyU5OSVCOC0lRTMlODElOEQtJUUzJTgxJUJGJUUzJTgxJTlBJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJUE4&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 癸 (Stem 10) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Guǐ * Korean: Gye (계) * Vietnamese: Quý * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoto (みずのと, 水の弟 – “Younger Water”)
== Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the {{ill|Water|en|Water|WD=Q283|lt=Water}} element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese|en|Chinese|WD=Q1173004|lt=Chinese}} chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten {{ill|carbon|en|carbon|WD=Q623|lt=carbon}} atoms, such as {{ill|decane|en|decane|WD=Q150717|lt=decane}} and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh (戊)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}. * The auspicious direction (Eho) associated with Kui is slightly south of due southeast (165 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoto == Pronunciations: 癸 (Stem 10) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Guǐ * Korean: Gye (계) * Vietnamese: Quý * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoto (みずのと, 水の弟 – “Younger Water”)
== Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the {{ill|Water|en|Water|WD=Q283|lt=Water}} element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese|en|Chinese|WD=Q1173004|lt=Chinese}} chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten {{ill|carbon|en|carbon|WD=Q623|lt=carbon}} atoms, such as {{ill|decane|en|decane|WD=Q150717|lt=decane}} and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Bō͘ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|zh|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}. * The auspicious direction (Eho) associated with Kui is slightly south of due southeast (165 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoto (癸)== {{wiktionary|癸}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Water Rooster|ja|癸酉|1=Yin Water Rooster|2=ja|3=癸酉|4=zh|5-癸酉|6=ko|7=계유|lt_ko=계유|8=fr|9=Coq de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Hahn|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rooster|zh|癸酉|1=Yin Water Rooster|2=ja|3=癸酉|4=zh|5-癸酉|6=ko|7=계유|lt_ko=계유|8=fr|9=Coq de == Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the {{ill|Water|en|Water|WD=Q283|lt=Water}} element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> {{ill|carbon|en|carbon|WD=Q623|lt=carbon}} atoms, such as {{ill|decane|en|decane|WD=Q150717|lt=decane}} and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Mizunoto''' (癸) is the 10th of the [[Ten Heavenly Stems]] (十干).<ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=56510&element_id=73054 <nowiki>Entry Details for 癸 [mizunoto] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=7c8f1fd0ee8d4469933a9f79651d6c92eea130421c8e1d4cc1e2c7a8335b6d1fJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=30e11164-4621-64c3-1cab-04d947776577&psq=Mizunoto+(%e7%99%b8)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU2MzU0L2tpLW1penVub3RvLSVFNyU5OSVCOC0lRTMlODElOEQtJUUzJTgxJUJGJUUzJTgxJTlBJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJUE4&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 癸 (Stem 10) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Guǐ * Korean: Gye (계) * Vietnamese: Quý * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoto (みずのと, 水の弟 – “Younger Water”)
== Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the {{ill|Water|en|Water|WD=Q283|lt=Water|1=Water}} element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese|en|Chinese|WD=Q1173004|lt=Chinese|1=Chinese}} chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten {{ill|carbon|en|carbon|WD=Q623|lt=carbon|1=Carbon}} atoms, such as {{ill|decane|en|decane|WD=Q150717|lt=decane|1=Decane}} and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Bō͘ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|zh|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}. * The auspicious direction (Eho) associated with Kui is slightly south of due southeast (165 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoto (癸)== {{wiktionary|癸}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Water Rooster|ja|癸酉|1=Yin Water Rooster|2=ja|3=癸酉|4=zh|5-癸酉|6=ko|7=계유|lt_ko=계유|8=fr|9=Coq de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Hahn|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rooster|zh|癸酉|1=Yin Water Rooster|2=ja|3=癸酉|4=zh|5-癸酉|6=ko|7=계유|lt_ko=계유|8=fr|9=Coq de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Hahn|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rooster|ko|계유|fr|Coq de eau|de|Wasser-Hahn|en|Water Rooster|lt=癸酉 (Yin Water Rooster)|lt_ko=癸酉 (계유)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rooster|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;" |{{ill|Yin Water Goat|ja|1=Yin Water Goat|2=ja|癸未|1=Yin Water Goat|zh|1=Yin Water Goat|2=ja|癸未|1=Yin Water Goat|ko|계미|fr|Chèvre de eau|de|Wasser-Schaf|en|Water Goat|lt=癸未 (Yin Water Goat)|lt_ko=癸未 (계미)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Goat|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Goat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- (계축)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Ox|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;" |{{ill|Yin Water Pig|ja|1=Yin Water Pig|2=ja|癸亥|zh|1=Yin Water Pig|2=ja|癸亥|ko|계해|fr|Cochon de eau|de|Wasser-Schwein|en|Water Pig|lt=癸亥 (Yin Water Pig)|lt_ko=癸亥 (계해)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Pig|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |}

Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> {{ill|carbon|en|carbon|WD=Q623|lt=carbon|1=Carbon}} atoms, such as {{ill|decane|en|decane|WD=Q150717|lt=decane|1=Decane}} and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh
