{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Mizunoto''' (癸) is the 10th of the [[Ten Heavenly Stems]] (十干).<ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=56510&element_id=73054 <nowiki>Entry Details for 癸 [mizunoto] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=7c8f1fd0ee8d4469933a9f79651d6c92eea130421c8e1d4cc1e2c7a8335b6d1fJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=30e11164-4621-64c3-1cab-04d947776577&psq=Mizunoto+(%e7%99%b8)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU2MzU0L2tpLW1penVub3RvLSVFNyU5OSVCOC0lRTMlODElOEQtJUUzJTgxJUJGJUUzJTgxJTlBJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJUE4&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 癸 (Stem 10) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Guǐ * Korean: Gye (계) * Vietnamese: Quý * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoto (みずのと, 水の弟 – “Younger Water”)
== Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the {{ill|Water|en|Water|WD=Q283|lt=Water|1=Water}} element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese|en|Chinese|WD=Q1173004|lt=Chinese|1=Chinese}} chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten {{ill|carbon|en|carbon|WD=Q623|lt=carbon|1=Carbon}} atoms, such as {{ill|decane|en|decane|WD=Q150717|lt=decane|1=Decane}} and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Bō͘ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|zh|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}. * The auspicious direction (Eho) associated with Kui is slightly south of due southeast (165 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoto (癸)== {{wiktionary|癸}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Water Rooster|ja|癸酉|1=Yin Water Rooster|2=ja|3=癸酉|4=zh|5-癸酉|6=ko|7=계유|lt_ko=계유|8=fr|9=Coq de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Hahn|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rooster|zh|癸酉|1=Yin Water Rooster|2=ja|3=癸酉|4=zh|5-癸酉|6=ko|7=계유|lt_ko=계유|8=fr|9=Coq de == Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the {{ill|Water|en|Water|WD=Q283|lt=Water|1=Water}} element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> {{ill|carbon|en|carbon|WD=Q623|lt=carbon|1=Carbon}} atoms, such as {{ill|decane|en|decane|WD=Q150717|lt=decane|1=Decane}} and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Mizunoto''' (癸) is the 10th of the [[Ten Heavenly Stems]] (十干).<ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=56510&element_id=73054 <nowiki>Entry Details for 癸 [mizunoto] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=7c8f1fd0ee8d4469933a9f79651d6c92eea130421c8e1d4cc1e2c7a8335b6d1fJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=30e11164-4621-64c3-1cab-04d947776577&psq=Mizunoto+(%e7%99%b8)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU2MzU0L2tpLW1penVub3RvLSVFNyU5OSVCOC0lRTMlODElOEQtJUUzJTgxJUJGJUUzJTgxJTlBJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJUE4&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 癸 (Stem 10) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Guǐ * Korean: Gye (계) * Vietnamese: Quý * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoto (みずのと, 水の弟 – “Younger Water”)
== Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the {{ill|Water|en|Water|WD=Q283|lt=Water|1=Water}} element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese|en|Chinese|WD=Q1173004|lt=Chinese|1=Chinese}} chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten {{ill|carbon|en|carbon|WD=Q623|lt=carbon|1=Carbon}} atoms, such as {{ill|decane|en|decane|WD=Q150717|lt=decane|1=Decane}} and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Bō͘ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|zh|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}. * The auspicious direction (Eho) associated with Kui is slightly south of due southeast (165 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoto (癸)== {{wiktionary|癸}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Water Rooster|ja|癸酉|1=Yin Water Rooster|2=ja|3=癸酉|4=zh|5-癸酉|6=ko|7=계유|lt_ko=계유|8=fr|9=Coq de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Hahn|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rooster|zh|癸酉|1=Yin Water Rooster|2=ja|3=癸酉|4=zh|5-癸酉|6=ko|7=계유|lt_ko=계유|8=fr|9=Coq de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Hahn|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rooster|ko|계유|fr|Coq de eau|de|Wasser-Hahn|en|Water Rooster|lt=癸酉 (Yin Water Rooster)|lt_ko=癸酉 (계유)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rooster|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;" |{{ill|Yin Water Goat|ja|1=Yin Water Goat|2=ja|癸未|1=Yin Water Goat|zh|1=Yin Water Goat|2=ja|癸未|1=Yin Water Goat|ko|계미|fr|Chèvre de eau|de|Wasser-Schaf|en|Water Goat|lt=癸未 (Yin Water Goat)|lt_ko=癸未 (계미)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Goat|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Goat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;" |{{ill|Yin Water Snake|ja|癸巳|zh|癸巳|ko|계사 (간지)|fr|Serpent de eau|de|Wasser-Schlange|en|Water Snake|lt=癸巳 (Yin Water Snake)|lt_ko=癸巳 (계사)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Snake|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |- style="text-align:left;" |{{ill|Yin Water Rabbit|ja|1=Yin Water Rabbit|2=ja|癸卯|1=Yin Water Rabbit|zh|1=Yin Water Rabbit|2=ja|癸卯|1=Yin Water link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;" |{{ill|Yin Water Ox|ja|1=Yin Water Ox|2=ja|癸丑|zh|1=Yin Water Ox|2=ja|癸丑|ko|계축|fr|Buffle de eau|de|Wasser-Büffel|en|Water Ox|lt=癸丑 (Yin Water Ox)|lt_ko=癸丑 (계축)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Ox|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;" |{{ill|Yin Water Pig|ja|1=Yin Water Pig|2=ja|癸亥|zh|1=Yin Water Pig|2=ja|癸亥|ko|계해|fr|Cochon de eau|de|Wasser-Schwein|en|Water Pig|lt=癸亥 (Yin Water Pig)|lt_ko=癸亥 (계해)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Pig|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |}

== Pronunciations: 癸 (Stem 10) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Guǐ * Korean: Gye (계) * Vietnamese: Quý * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Mizunoto (みずのと, 水の弟 – “Younger Water”)
== Overview == * In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]] (陰陽五行説), Kui is assigned to the Yin aspect of the {{ill|Water|en|Water|qid=Q283|lt=Water|1=Water}} element. This is why in Japan it is also referred to as "mizunoto" (水の弟), meaning "younger brother of water." * The origin of the Chinese character "癸" has seen various proposals over time, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=New Evidence on Shuowen Jiezi| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| pages=969–70|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotated "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| pages=102–3|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Reinterpreting Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Publishing House| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=111–2|qq=}}</ref> The use of "癸" to denote the tenth position in the Heavenly Stems is a result of phonetic loaning. * Years ending with the digit 3 in the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} correspond to Kui years. Additionally, its alternative name when representing a year is "Zhaoyang" (昭陽).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [Zhaoyang : ㄓㄠ ㄧㄤˋ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=115346&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education, Revised Mandarin Dictionary, 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese|en|Chinese|qid=Q1173004|lt=Chinese|1=Chinese}} chemical nomenclature, "kui" is used for compounds and [[:en:Functional groups|Functional groups]] containing ten {{ill|carbon|en|carbon|qid=Q623|lt=carbon|1=Carbon}} atoms, such as {{ill|decane|en|decane|qid=Q150717|lt=decane|1=Decane}} and [[:en:decyl group|decyl group]]. * The opposite of Kui in the Heavenly Stems is {{ill|Tsuchinoe (戊)|cdo|Muô (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Wu (tige céleste)|gan|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|hak|Vú (thiên-kôn)|ja|Tsuchinoe|ko|무 (천간)|vi|Mậu (Thiên can)|za|Fouh (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Bō͘ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|zh|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}. * The auspicious direction (Eho) associated with Kui is slightly south of due southeast (165 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Mizunoto (癸)== {{wiktionary|癸}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Water Rooster|ja|癸酉|1=Yin Water Rooster|2=ja|3=癸酉|4=zh|5-癸酉|6=ko|7=계유|lt_ko=계유|8=fr|9=Coq de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Hahn|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Water Rooster|zh|癸酉|1=Yin Water Rooster|2=ja|3=癸酉|4=zh|5-癸酉|6=ko|7=계유|lt_ko=계유|8=fr|9=Coq de Water Rooster|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;" |{{ill|Yin Water Goat|ja|1=Yin Water Goat|2=ja|癸未|1=Yin Water Goat|zh|1=Yin Water Goat|2=ja|癸未|1=Yin Water Goat|ko|계미|fr|Chèvre de {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Mizunoto''' (癸) is the 10th of the [[Ten Heavenly Stems]] (十干).<ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=56510&element_id=73054 <nowiki>Entry Details for 癸 [mizunoto] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=7c8f1fd0ee8d4469933a9f79651d6c92eea130421c8e1d4cc1e2c7a8335b6d1fJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=30e11164-4621-64c3-1cab-04d947776577&psq=Mizunoto+(%e7%99%b8)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU2MzU0L2tpLW1penVub3RvLSVFNyU5OSVCOC0lRTMlODElOEQtJUUzJTgxJUJGJUUzJTgxJTlBJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJUE4&ntb=1</ref>
